result:
Reviewing the supplied C-LDIP-01 artifacts (source index + no-omission matrix, with the two plans as context) and returning a read-only JSON verdict; all listed artifacts are treated as present per the packet, and findings are derived solely from the supplied content.

```json
  "review_id": "C-LDIP-01",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-8",
  "effort": "max",

  "scope_note": "Verdict anchored on the fully-supplied ldip-source-index.md and ldip-no-omission-coverage-matrix.md. The two plans were provided as excerpts and used only as corroborating context; no filesystem state was inferred and no artifact is treated as missing.",

    "q1_sections_1_to_23_no_silent_omission": "PASS. All 23 top-level sections present. Line ranges are continuous and non-overlapping from line 7 to line 1342 with no gaps; section 23 ends at 1342, matching the expected 1,342-line count, and the index's claimed SHA256 matches the packet's expected value.",
    "q2_lineranges_families_rp_anchors_plausible": "PASS_WITH_FINDINGS. Line ranges internally consistent; RP anchors in the index are consistent and plausible against the matrix (Clean Room→RP20/16/26, Agents→RP17/18, Tools→RP17/18/27/03, Search→RP07/15/17, UI→RP21+, NFR→RP16/26/29, API→RP22/23/27, Compliance→RP16/24/26, Risk→RP00/02/16/17/20/26 all match). Family-assignment consistency has defects: see F1, F2.",
    "q3_named_object_classes_preserved": "PASS_WITH_FINDINGS. Entities (28), table field families (7 tables), agents (12), tool tiers (T0-T7), clean room types (7), shared objects (8), UI surfaces (5), NFR IDs (SEC/PERF/AVAIL/DQ/AIQ), AI+data metrics, tests (T-SEC/T-DOC/T-CON/T-DD/T-AI), priorities (MVP/2nd/3rd wave), and risks (10) are all enumerated. APIs and events are the one class captured only at family level, not enumerated by name: see F3.",
    "q4_no_premature_implementation_or_production_ready": "PASS. Both artifacts carry explicit planning-only status disclaiming implementation, contract change, weighted-ledger change, validator change, and production_ready. Matrix Live Repo Baseline states LDIP is a 'source-indexed planning overlay' not yet a weighted-ledger source unit. No premature claims found.",
    "q5_bolt_on_late_risk_areas": "PASS_WITH_FINDINGS. Primary bolt-on risk is the source-connector layer (F2): LDIP-SRC is a declared family with RP anchors but has no source-section trace, so connector requirements (DMS/email/calendar/timesheet/billing/CRM/DD/law-DB) lack an extractable anchor. Secondary risk is un-enumerated APIs/events (F3). Architecture section 16 is under-decomposed (F4)."
  },

  "strengths": [
    "Complete 1-23 section coverage with fully continuous line ranges totaling 1342, matching expected line count and claimed SHA256.",
    "Strong planning-only discipline; no implementation/ledger/validator/production_ready claims.",
    "Thorough named-object enumeration for entities, tables, agents, tools, clean rooms, UI, NFRs, metrics, tests, priorities, and risks.",
    "RP anchors are internally consistent between source index and no-omission matrix and plausible against the Law Firm OS RP structure."
  ],

  "findings": [
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-01-F1",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "Source index uses LDIP-AUDIT family that is undefined in the no-omission family taxonomy",
      "evidence": "ldip-source-index.md section 11.2 (Tool Call 로그, lines 746-766) is tagged coverage family LDIP-AUDIT, but ldip-no-omission-coverage-matrix.md Coverage Families table defines only 20 families (GOAL, ARCH, SRC, CAT, DOC, ING, SEC, SRCH, AGT, TOOL, CLEAN, UI, NFR, DQ, COMP, API, OUT, TEST, RISK, PRIO) and the integration-plan candidate ID scheme lists the same 20; neither defines LDIP-AUDIT. Audit capability itself remains covered via LDIP-TOOL tool-call-log fields, the RP03 anchor, the Audit Event entity, and the Audit-First invariant, so this is a taxonomy-consistency defect rather than a capability omission, but it breaks the 'classified exactly once against a fixed family set' coverage rule.",
      "recommended_action": "Adopt one authoritative family set across all LDIP planning docs: either add LDIP-AUDIT (with RP03 anchor and must-not-miss audit objects) to the matrix and candidate ID scheme, or retag 11.2 to LDIP-TOOL/LDIP-SEC and fold audit into an existing family."

    {

      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "LDIP-SRC source-connector family is declared in the matrix but anchored to no source-index section",

Scanning every family cell in the ldip-source-index.md section table, no row carries LDIP-SRC; the sections that would host connectors (3.2 lines 85-99; 5.1.1 lines 219-237; 16 lines 992-1056) are tagged only LDIP-ARCH/GOAL/PRIO. The source-connector class therefore has no section-level trace anchor, which is precisely the late bolt-on risk in Review Question 5 and would leave RP01/RP08/RP22/RP23 connector work under-extracted.",
      "recommended_action": "Assign LDIP-SRC to the appropriate source section(s) (likely 3.2, 5.1.1, and/or 16) in the source index and add an explicit connector line family so each source connector becomes an extractable candidate with a real source-section anchor before RP01 planning."
    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-01-F3",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "APIs and events preserved only at family level, not enumerated as named objects",
      "evidence": "Review Question 3 requires APIs and events to be preserved as named object classes. The matrix enumerates by name agents (12), tool tiers (T0-T7), clean room types (7), shared objects (8), UI surfaces (5), NFR IDs, metrics, and tests, but for source section 17 (API 및 이벤트 사양, lines 1057-1109) it captures only family LDIP-API with must-not-miss properties 'idempotency, auditability, event naming' and lists no individual endpoints or named event types. Specific APIs/events can therefore be silently dropped during extraction, weakening the no-omission guarantee for this one class.",
      "recommended_action": "Add an explicit API and Event enumeration to the no-omission matrix (named endpoints and named event types from section 17), mirroring the agent/tool/clean-room style, so C-LDIP-02 extraction has a complete API/event checklist."
    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-01-F4",
      "severity": "P3",
      "title": "Subsection-decomposition asymmetry between sections 1-14 and 15-23",
      "evidence": "ldip-source-index.md decomposes sections 1-14 into subsections but lists 15-23 as atomic rows. Most are mitigated by matrix enumeration (15 compliance, 18 outputs, 19 metrics, 20 tests, 21 priorities, 22 risks), but the large technical-architecture section 16 (lines 992-1056) carries a single LDIP-ARCH tag with no sub-rows, so its internal items (storage, search/index infrastructure, model serving, deployment, vendor-neutral core) are not individually visible for trace.",
      "recommended_action": "Add subsection rows or a brief sub-item list for the larger late sections, especially 16, so architecture sub-items are individually anchored. Non-blocking reviewability improvement."
    }
  ],

  "gate_rationale": {
    "planning_gate": "Not blocked. Verdict is PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with only P2/P3 findings; per the project's P2 rule these 'can remain in planning with explicit correction,' so C-LDIP-01 can clear the planning gate once F1-F3 are adjudicated and tracked.",
    "implementation_gate": "Blocked until cleared. Three open P2 findings exist, and P2 is defined as 'must be fixed before implementation.' All three are planning-document trace fixes (no code), so the gate clears by adjudicating and applying F1-F3 to the source index and matrix.",

```

Summary: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS — the source index covers all 23 sections with consistent line ranges and strong planning-only discipline; the three P2 items (LDIP-AUDIT/LDIP-SRC family-taxonomy mismatch and un-enumerated APIs/events) are documentation-trace fixes that gate implementation but not continued planning.
